name: Biscochitos- a Cut Out Sugar Cookie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. In a large bowl, mix together the sugar and margarine (or butter). Add the sweet sherry and egg, stirring until well combined.
3. Gradually add the remaining ingredients (flour, baking powder, crushed anise seeds, salt, additional sugar, and cinnamon) to the mixture. Stir until a dough forms.
4. Divide the dough into two equal balls. Roll each ball out on a floured surface to about 1/4 inch (0.5 cm) thick.
5. Use cookie cutters to cut the rolled dough into desired shapes. Place the cookies on an ungreased baking sheet.
6. Mix together the remaining sugar and cinnamon in a small bowl. Sprinkle this mixture over the cookies before placing them in the oven.
7. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until golden brown. Allow the cookies to cool on the baking sheet for a few minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely.
These cookies are traditionally made into cactus shapes, but you can create any desired shape using cookie cutters.
